title: comocladia dodonaea
intro: Comocladia dodonaea, also known as the West Indian sandalwood or Cuban sandalwood, is a tropical tree native to the Caribbean and Central America. It is valued for its aromatic heartwood and is traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Comocladia dodonaea have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and combat infections. The plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and reduce stress.
